lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: When shall we come to the top of that same hill?

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: You do climb up it now: look, how we labour.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Methinks the ground is even.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Horrible steep. Hark, do you hear the sea?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: No, truly.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Why, then, your other senses grow imperfect

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: By your eyes’ anguish.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: So may it be, indeed:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Methinks thy voice is alter’d; and thou speak’st

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: In better phrase and matter than thou didst.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: You’re much deceived: in nothing am I changed

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: But in my garments.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Methinks you’re better spoken.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Come on, sir; here’s the place: stand still. How fearful

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And dizzy ’tis, to cast one’s eyes so low!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: The crows and choughs that wing the midway air

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Show scarce so gross as beetles: half way down

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Hangs one that gathers samphire, dreadful trade!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Methinks he seems no bigger than his head:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: The fishermen, that walk upon the beach,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Appear like mice; and yond tall anchoring bark,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Diminish’d to her cock; her cock, a buoy

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Almost too small for sight: the murmuring surge,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: That on the unnumber’d idle pebbles chafes,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Cannot be heard so high. I’ll look no more;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Lest my brain turn, and the deficient sight

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Topple down headlong.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Set me where you stand.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Give me your hand: you are now within a foot

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Of the extreme verge: for all beneath the moon

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Would I not leap upright.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Let go my hand.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Here, friend, ’s another purse; in it a jewel

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Well worth a poor man’s taking: fairies and gods

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Prosper it with thee! Go thou farther off;

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Bid me farewell, and let me hear thee going.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Now fare you well, good sir.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: With all my heart.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Why I do trifle thus with his despair Is done to cure it.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O you mighty gods!

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: This world I do renounce, and, in your sights,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Shake patiently my great affliction off:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: If I could bear it longer, and not fall

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To quarrel with your great opposeless wills,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: My snuff and loathed part of nature should

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Burn itself out. If Edgar live, O, bless him!

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Now, fellow, fare thee well. He falls forward.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Gone, sir: farewell.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And yet I know not how conceit may rob

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: The treasury of life, when life itself

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Yields to the theft: had he been where he thought,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: By this, had thought been past. Alive or dead?

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Ho, you sir! friend! Hear you, sir! speak!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Thus might he pass indeed: yet he revives.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: What are you, sir?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Away, and let me die.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Hadst thou been aught but gossamer, feathers, air,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: So many fathom down precipitating,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Thou’dst shiver’d like an egg: but thou dost breathe;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Hast heavy substance; bleed’st not; speak’st; art sound.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Ten masts at each make not the altitude

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Which thou hast perpendicularly fell:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Thy life’s a miracle. Speak yet again.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: But have I fall’n, or no?

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: From the dread summit of this chalky bourn.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Look up a-height; the shrill-gorged lark so far

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Cannot be seen or heard: do but look up.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Alack, I have no eyes.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Is wretchedness deprived that benefit,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To end itself by death? ’Twas yet some comfort,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: When misery could beguile the tyrant’s rage,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: And frustrate his proud will.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Give me your arm: Up: so. How is ’t? Feel you your legs? You stand.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Too well, too well.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: This is above all strangeness.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Upon the crown o’ the cliff, what thing was that

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Which parted from you?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: A poor unfortunate beggar.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: As I stood here below, methought his eyes

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Were two full moons; he had a thousand noses,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Horns whelk’d and waved like the enridged sea:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: It was some fiend; therefore, thou happy father,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Think that the clearest gods, who make them honours

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Of men’s impossibilities, have preserved thee.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I do remember now: henceforth I’ll bear

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Affliction till it do cry out itself

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: “Enough, enough,” and die. That thing you speak of,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I took it for a man; often ’twould say

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: “The fiend, the fiend:” he led me to that place.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Bear free and patient thoughts. But who comes here?

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: No, they cannot touch me for coining; I am the king himself.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: O thou side-piercing sight!

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Nature’s above art in that respect. There’s your press-money. That fellow handles

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: his bow like a crow-keeper: draw me a clothier’s yard. Look, look,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: a mouse! Peace, peace; this piece of toasted cheese will do ’t.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: There’s my gauntlet; I’ll prove it on a giant. Bring up the

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: brown bills. O, well flown, bird! i’ the clout, i’ the clout:

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: hewgh! Give the word.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Sweet marjoram.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Pass.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I know that voice.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Ha! Goneril, with a white beard! They flattered me like a dog;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: and told me I had white hairs in my beard ere the

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: black ones were there. To say “ay” and “no” to every thing

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: that I said!—“Ay” and “no” too was no good divinity. When the

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: rain came to wet me once, and the wind to make me

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: chatter; when the thunder would not peace at my bidding; there I

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: found ’em, there I smelt ’em out. Go to, they are not

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: men o’ their words: they told me I was every thing; ’tis

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: a lie, I am not ague-proof.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: The trick of that voice I do well remember:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Is ’t not the king?

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Ay, every inch a king:

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: When I do stare, see how the subject quakes.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: I pardon that man’s life. What was thy cause? Adultery?

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Thou shalt not die: die for adultery! No:

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: The wren goes to ’t, and the small gilded fly

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Does lecher in my sight.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Let copulation thrive; for Gloucester’s bastard son

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Was kinder to his father than my daughters

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Got ’tween the lawful sheets.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: To ’t, luxury, pell-mell! for I lack soldiers.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Behold yond simpering dame,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Whose face between her forks presages snow;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: That minces virtue, and does shake the head

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: To hear of pleasure’s name;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: The fitchew, nor the soiled horse, goes to ’t

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: With a more riotous appetite.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Down from the waist they are Centaurs,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Though women all above:

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: But to the girdle do the gods inherit,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Beneath is all the fiends’;

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O, let me kiss that hand!

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Let me wipe it first; it smells of mortality.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: O ruin’d piece of nature! This great world

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Shall so wear out to nought. Dost thou know me?

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: I remember thine eyes well enough. Dost thou squiny at me? No,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: do thy worst, blind Cupid! I’ll not love. Read thou this challenge;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: mark but the penning of it.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Were all the letters suns, I could not see one.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I would not take this from report; it is,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And my heart breaks at it.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Read.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: What, with the case of eyes?

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: O, ho, are you there with me? No eyes in your head,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: nor no money in your purse? Your eyes are in a heavy

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: case, your purse in a light; yet you see how this world

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: goes.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: I see it feelingly.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: What, art mad? A man may see how this world goes with

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: no eyes. Look with thine ears: see how yond justice rails upon

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: yond simple thief. Hark, in thine ear: change places; and, handy-dandy, which

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: is the justice, which is the thief? Thou hast seen a farmer’s

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: dog bark at a beggar?

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Ay, sir.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: And the creature run from the cur? There thou mightst behold the

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: great image of authority: a dog’s obeyed in office.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: O, matter and impertinency mix’d! Reason in madness!

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: If thou wilt weep my fortunes, take my eyes.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: I know thee well enough; thy name is Gloucester:

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Thou must be patient; we came crying hither:

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Thou know’st, the first time that we smell the air,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: We wawl and cry. I will preach to thee: mark.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Alack, alack the day!

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: When we are born, we cry that we are come

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: To this great stage of fools: this a good block;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: It were a delicate stratagem, to shoe

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: A troop of horse with felt: I’ll put ’t in proof;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: And when I have stol’n upon these sons-in-law,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Then, kill, kill, kill, kill, kill, kill!

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: O, here he is: lay hand upon him. Sir,

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Your most dear daughter—

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: No rescue? What, a prisoner? I am even

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: The natural fool of fortune. Use me well;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: You shall have ransom. Let me have surgeons;

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: I am cut to the brains.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: You shall have any thing.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: No seconds? all myself?

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Why, this would make a man a man of salt,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: To use his eyes for garden water-pots,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Ay, and laying autumn’s dust.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Good sir—

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: I will die bravely, like a bridegroom. What!

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: I will be jovial: come, come; I am a king,

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: My masters, know you that.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: You are a royal one, and we obey you.

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: Then there’s life in’t. Nay, if you get it, you shall get

lines.speaker: KING LEAR
lines.text: it with running. Sa, sa, sa, sa.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: A sight most pitiful in the meanest wretch,

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Past speaking of in a king! Thou hast one daughter,

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Who redeems nature from the general curse

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Which twain have brought her to.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Hail, gentle sir.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Sir, speed you: what’s your will?

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Do you hear aught, sir, of a battle toward?

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Most sure and vulgar: every one hears that,

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Which can distinguish sound.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: But, by your favour,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: How near’s the other army?

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Near and on speedy foot; the main descry

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Stands on the hourly thought.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I thank you, sir: that’s all.

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Though that the queen on special cause is here,

lines.speaker: GENTLEMAN
lines.text: Her army is moved on.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I thank you, sir.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: You ever-gentle gods, take my breath from me:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Let not my worser spirit tempt me again

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To die before you please!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Well pray you, father.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Now, good sir, what are you?

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: A most poor man, made tame to fortune’s blows;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Who, by the art of known and feeling sorrows,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Am pregnant to good pity. Give me your hand,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I’ll lead you to some biding.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Hearty thanks:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: The bounty and the benison of heaven

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: To boot, and boot!

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: A proclaim’d prize! Most happy!

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: That eyeless head of thine was first framed flesh

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: To raise my fortunes. Thou old unhappy traitor,

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Briefly thyself remember: the sword is out

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: That must destroy thee.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Now let thy friendly hand

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Put strength enough to’t. Edgar interposes.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Wherefore, bold peasant,

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Darest thou support a publish’d traitor? Hence;

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Lest that the infection of his fortune take

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Like hold on thee. Let go his arm.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Ch’ill not let go, zir, without vurther ’casion.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Let go, slave, or thou diest!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Good gentleman, go your gait, and let poor volk pass. An chud

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: ha’ bin zwaggered out of my life, ’twould not ha’ bin zo

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: long as ’tis by a vortnight. Nay, come not near th’ old

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: man; keep out, che vor ye, or ise try whether your costard

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: or my ballow be the harder: ch’ill be plain with you.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Out, dunghill!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Ch’ill pick your teeth, zir: come; no matter vor your foins. They

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: fight, and Edgar knocks him down.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Slave, thou hast slain me: villain, take my purse:

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: If ever thou wilt thrive, bury my body;

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: And give the letters which thou find’st about me

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: To Edmund earl of Gloucester; seek him out

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Upon the British party: O, untimely death!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I know thee well: a serviceable villain;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: As duteous to the vices of thy mistress

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: As badness would desire.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: What, is he dead?

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Sit you down, father; rest you

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Let’s see these pockets: the letters that he speaks of

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: May be my friends. He’s dead; I am only sorry

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: He had no other death’s-man. Let us see:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Leave, gentle wax; and, manners, blame us not:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: To know our enemies’ minds, we’ld rip their hearts;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Their papers, is more lawful.

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: The king is mad: how stiff is my vile sense,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: That I stand up, and have ingenious feeling

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: Of my huge sorrows! Better I were distract:

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: So should my thoughts be sever’d from my griefs,

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: And woes by wrong imaginations lose

lines.speaker: GLOUCESTER
lines.text: The knowledge of themselves.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Give me your hand:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Far off, methinks, I hear the beaten drum:

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Come, father, I’ll bestow you with a friend.

stage_directions.text: Enter Gloucester, and Edgar dressed like a peasant.
Enter King Lear, fantastically dressed with wild flowers.
The safer sense will ne’er accommodate His master thus.

There’s hell, there’s darkness, there’s the sulphurous pit, Burning, scalding, stench, consumption; fie, fie, fie! pah, pah! Give me an ounce of civet, good apothecary, to sweeten my imagination: there’s money for thee.

Thou rascal beadle, hold thy bloody hand! Why dost thou lash that whore? Strip thine own back; Thou hotly lust’st to use her in that kind For which thou whipp’st her. The usurer hangs the cozener. Through tatter’d clothes small vices do appear; Robes and furr’d gowns hide all. Plate sin with gold, And the strong lance of justice hurtless breaks: Arm it in rags, a pygmy’s straw does pierce it. None does offend, none, I say, none; I’ll able ’em: Take that of me, my friend, who have the power To seal the accuser’s lips. Get thee glass eyes; And like a scurvy politician, seem To see the things thou dost not. Now, now, now, now: Pull off my boots: harder, harder: so.

Reads. “Let our reciprocal vows be remembered. You have many opportunities to cut him off: if your will want not, time and place will be fruitfully offered. There is nothing done, if he return the conqueror: then am I the prisoner, and his bed my goal; from the loathed warmth whereof deliver me, and supply the place for your labour.

O undistinguish’d space of woman’s will! A plot upon her virtuous husband’s life; And the exchange my brother! Here, in the sands, Thee I’ll rake up, the post unsanctified Of murderous lechers: and in the mature time With this ungracious paper strike the sight Of the death practised duke: for him ’tis well That of thy death and business I can tell.

glosses.definition: Gloucester believes he's climbing toward the cliff — Edgar is leading him on level ground, reshaping reality by voice
vertiginous — Edgar's described cliff makes even the reader dizzy
red-legged Cornish crows — the cliff-birds Edgar describes
halfway between top and bottom — the birds look small as beetles
as big as beetles — the birds diminished by distance
a cliff-growing herb (Crithmum maritimum) — collected by men dangling on ropes, the figure makes vertigo
the terrifying occupation of the samphire-gatherer
that tall ship at anchor, now looking small
cock-boat — small boat, reduced to a speck by distance
countless restless stones — of the beach
Edgar breaks off his cliff-description, feigning vertigo
Gloucester's grief — justification of his intended suicide
the unopposable will (of the gods)
the stub of a candle — Gloucester's life reduced to this
evil impulse

glosses.phrase: ten masts at each make not the altitude
glosses.definition: ten ships' masts stacked would not reach the height — Edgar's lie reinforced
fine spider-silk — which normally floats down; Gloucester should have shattered by now
you still have your life — Edgar's therapeutic theater
cliff-top — Gloucester is persuaded he fell from there
edge; boundary (of the cliff)
Gloucester's new stoicism — Edgar has walked him through suicide and out the other side
Gloucester thinks a demon led him to the cliff — Edgar lets this stand
the most glorious / just divinities — Edgar's retroactive theology
heart-piercing view — Edgar on seeing Lear enter in flower-crown

annotations.title: The non-existent cliff — Edgar's therapeutic theater
annotations.body: Edgar leads blind Gloucester to what Gloucester believes is the edge of the Dover cliffs, describes a vertiginous view that pure language makes real, and stands by while Gloucester 'falls' onto flat ground. This is Shakespeare's most sustained demonstration of theater's power to construct reality through verbal description alone. The cliff exists only in Edgar's speech. The ekphrasis — choughs 'midway air,' samphire-gatherers 'dreadful trade,' fishermen 'like mice,' the anchored ship 'diminish'd to her cock' — is vertiginous prose-verse, designed to make the reader's head spin alongside Gloucester's. After Gloucester falls, Edgar becomes a different speaker, 'of the bottom,' convincing Gloucester that a fiend led him and that the gods have preserved him. The scene is Edgar's therapeutic lie: he manages his father's despair by staging a survival miracle. That Shakespeare grants this theater its healing work (Gloucester's 'henceforth I'll bear / Affliction till it do cry out itself / Enough') is a quiet endorsement of theater as consolation — the opposite of Hamlet's bitter view of performance.
sources: Jonathan Bate, The Genius of Shakespeare (1997)

annotations.title: 'Great stage of fools' — the theatrum mundi tradition
annotations.body: Lear's 'When we are born, we cry that we are come / To this great stage of fools' enters the theatrum mundi tradition at its most pessimistic. The metaphor of the world as a stage has a long history — Plato, Epictetus, Erasmus (whose 'The Praise of Folly' Shakespeare knew), Petronius ('mundus universus exercet histrionicam,' the whole world plays the actor). Shakespeare himself returns to it constantly ('All the world's a stage' in As You Like It; 'We are such stuff as dreams are made on' in The Tempest). Lear's contribution is the quality of the actors: not all human beings, just fools. Birth itself is figured as an entry into absurdity. This is pitch-dark — Lear in full disillusion — but it is also philosophically serious. The play ends with Edgar (or Albany) saying 'Speak what we feel, not what we ought to say,' which keeps the theatrical frame ('say') while endorsing sincerity over performance within it. The world is still a stage; only the register of the performance is negotiable.
sources: Frances Yates, Theatre of the World (1969)
Stephen Greenblatt, 'Shakespeare and the Ethics of Authority' (2010)

annotations.title: 'Reason in madness' — Edgar's formulation of the play's central paradox
annotations.body: Edgar's aside during Lear's mad coherence — 'O, matter and impertinency mix'd! / Reason in madness!' — is the play's key formulation of its central paradox. Lear on the heath and at Dover has been speaking wisdom through derangement. 'Matter' (substance, real content) is mixed with 'impertinency' (off-topic ravings); the signal rides on noise. Edgar, who has spent much of the play playing at madness as Poor Tom, is the right character to recognise this. 'Reason in madness' will become a touchstone phrase in the play's critical tradition — E.M. Forster quoted it; Kurosawa's Ran takes its title from the Japanese word for chaos or turmoil that shares semantic territory with 'reason in madness.' The phrase names Shakespeare's deepest gamble in the play: that truth is accessible to the broken in ways the composed cannot reach.
sources: Harold Bloom, Shakespeare: The Invention of the Human (1998)
E.M. Forster, Aspects of the Novel (1927)

trials.prompt: What does Edgar accomplish by staging the non-existent cliff at Dover?
options: He uses pure verbal description to construct a reality Gloucester can 'fall' from — theater's power to heal by making a survival miracle out of a flat field
He tests whether Gloucester is faking blindness

trials.prompt: Lear's 'great stage of fools' belongs to which long tradition?
options: Theatrum mundi — 'the world as theater,' a tradition running from Plato through Erasmus and Petronius to Shakespeare's own 'All the world's a stage'
The play's central paradox — that truth is accessible to the broken in ways the composed cannot reach; signal riding on noise

trials.prompt: The extensive verbal description of the Dover cliff (choughs, samphire, fishermen) serves what purpose?
options: Constructing reality through language alone — the cliff exists only in Edgar's speech; the ekphrasis is designed to make reader and Gloucester equally vertiginous
